lines.speaker: Frege
lines.text: We now have all four quantifier rules: introduction and elimination for both ∀ and ∃.

lines.speaker: De Morgan
lines.text: They form a closed system. You can introduce a universal from an arbitrary instance, eliminate it to any term. You can introduce an existential from a witness, and eliminate it by opening a temporary name.

lines.speaker: Frege
lines.text: The symmetry is beautiful. But notice the asymmetry: ∀-elim is unrestricted — you may substitute any term. ∃-elim, however, demands that the witness variable not escape into the conclusion.

lines.speaker: De Morgan
lines.text: That restriction is the price we pay for reasoning about "some" without knowing which one.

lines.speaker: Frege
lines.text: Exactly. It is a controlled leak of information. The variable is a placeholder — not a promise.
